commonMedications.name: Semaglutide / GLP-1 (Ozempic/Wegovy) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D covers for diabetes (tier 3). $25 copay typical. Without coverage ~$900/mo. IRA negotiated price TBD.

commonMedications.name: Levothyroxine (Synthroid) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Montelukast (Singulair) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Hydrochlorothiazide (HCTZ) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Olmesartan/Medoxomil (Benicar) [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Albuterol inhaler [P1]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D tier 2; $10-25 copay. IRA cap applies.

commonMedications.name: Metformin 500mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Allopurinol (gout) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Lisinopril [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Topiramate (Topamax) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1-2; $4-15 copay

commonMedications.name: Metoprolol (Lopressor) [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

commonMedications.name: Atorvastatin 20mg [P2]
commonMedications.coverageNote: Medicare Part D generic tier 1; $0-10 copay

medicine.pharmacyAccess: Excellent. CVS, Walgreens, Walmart, Costco pharmacies widely available throughout Northern Virginia. Mail-order options through Medicare Part D plans (OptumRx, Express Scripts, Caremark).
medicine.prescriptionSystem: Medicare Part D prescription drug plan. Choose standalone PDP or get coverage through Medicare Advantage. Annual out-of-pocket cap $2,000 under Inflation Reduction Act.
medicine.insuranceCoverage: Medicare Part D covers most prescriptions with tiered copays. Generic drugs $0-15/month, preferred brands $30-50/month, specialty drugs 25-33% coinsurance up to $2,000 annual cap.

sources.title: Inflation Reduction Act Drug Provisions - KFF

annualExamCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine vision exams. Must have separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision.

vision.insuranceCoverage: Need separate vision plan or Medicare Advantage with vision. Standalone vision plans (VSP, EyeMed) run $15-25/month and cover annual exam + $100-200 frame/lens allowance.
vision.specialistWaitTime: 1-3 weeks for routine eye exams in Northern Virginia. Shorter waits at retail optometry (LensCrafters, Walmart Vision). Ophthalmologist referrals 2-4 weeks.
vision.seniorConsiderations: Medicare covers cataract surgery and glaucoma screening. Annual glaucoma screening covered for high-risk beneficiaries. Diabetic retinopathy screening covered. One pair of eyeglasses or contacts after cataract surgery.

annualCleaningCost.notes: Original Medicare does NOT cover routine dental. Need separate dental plan or Medicare Advantage with dental.

dental.insuranceCoverage: Need separate dental plan ($20-50/month) or Medicare Advantage with dental. Standalone plans (Delta Dental, Cigna) typically cover 100% preventive, 50-80% basic, 50% major procedures. Annual maximums $1,000-2,000.
dental.dentistAvailability: Excellent in Northern Virginia. Large number of dental practices, short wait times (1-2 weeks for routine). Many dentists accept dental discount plans for uninsured seniors.

categories.name: Dining Out
categories.seniorDiscounts: Many restaurants offer 10-15% senior discounts. AARP members get discounts at Denny's, Outback, and others.
categories.notes: Lunch for 2 at moderate restaurant ~$35 + 6% tax + 20% tip = ~$44/meal, 2x/week (~8.7/mo) = ~$355/mo

categories.name: Cable + 1 Gig Internet
categories.seniorDiscounts: Some providers offer senior plans. Xfinity 5-year price lock at $60/mo available.
categories.notes: Xfinity 1 Gig $60-70/mo or Verizon FiOS $75/mo. No data caps.

categories.name: Amazon Prime
categories.seniorDiscounts: Medicaid recipients qualify for Prime at $6.99/mo.
categories.notes: $14.99/mo includes Prime Video, free shipping, Prime Reading.

categories.name: Gym/Fitness
categories.seniorDiscounts: SilverSneakers free gym membership through many Medicare Advantage plans.

categories.name: Parks/Outdoors
categories.seniorDiscounts: America the Beautiful Senior Pass $20/year (lifetime $80) for national parks.

categories.name: Books/Library
categories.seniorDiscounts: Fairfax County Library system free. Senior book clubs and digital resources.

categories.name: Coffee/Cafes
categories.seniorDiscounts: McDonald's and Dunkin' offer senior coffee discounts in some locations.

monthlyPass.notes: WMATA Metro Senior SmarTrip card gives half-fare during off-peak. Fairfax Connector senior fare $0.85.

publicTransit.coverage: WMATA Metro covers major NoVA corridors (Orange/Silver/Blue lines). Fairfax Connector buses serve suburban areas. VRE commuter rail connects Fredericksburg/Manassas to DC.
publicTransit.seniorDiscount: Seniors 65+ get half-fare on WMATA Metro during off-peak hours and all day on buses. Must have Senior SmarTrip card.

rideShare.availability: Excellent throughout Northern Virginia. Uber and Lyft widely available 24/7.
rideShare.notes: Uber/Lyft prices surge during rush hours. GoGoGrandparent service available for seniors who prefer phone-based ride ordering.

registration.notes: Virginia charges personal property tax on vehicles, assessed by locality. Fairfax County rate $4.57 per $100 of assessed value. On a $25,000 car this is ~$1,140/year. State vehicle registration $33-53/year.

insurance.notes: Virginia has higher-than-average car insurance rates. NoVA premiums higher due to traffic density. Senior safe driver discounts available (5-15%).

fuel.notes: ~$3.50/gallon average in Virginia. VA gas tax $0.294/gallon. Typical senior drives 8,000-12,000 miles/year.

parking.notes: Free parking in most suburban areas. Apartment complexes typically include 1 spot. Metro station parking $4.95-5.75/day.

tolls.notes: I-66 Inside the Beltway tolling, Dulles Toll Road ($3.25-6.50), I-495 Express Lanes (variable pricing). E-ZPass recommended. Monthly cost varies greatly by commute pattern.

carOwnership.seniorDiscounts: Virginia Safe Driver discount for completing AARP Smart Driver course (5-15% insurance reduction). Some counties offer vehicle property tax relief for seniors.

transportation.walkability: Low to moderate in Northern Virginia suburbs. Walkable areas near Metro stations (Arlington, Tysons). Most of Fairfax County requires a car for daily errands.

cycling.infrastructure: Capital Bikeshare available near Metro stations in Arlington/Alexandria. Protected bike lanes expanding in urban areas. Most suburbs have limited cycling infrastructure.
cycling.notes: Capital Bikeshare single trip $2, day pass $8, annual $95.

electricVehicle.purchaseNotes: Tesla Model 3 ~$35K, Model Y ~$45K, Hyundai Ioniq 5 ~$42K, Chevy Equinox EV ~$28K. Federal tax credit up to $7,500 (income limits apply). Some states add incentives.
electricVehicle.incentiveNotes: Federal EV tax credit up to $7,500 under IRA (income < $150K single / $300K joint). Must be new vehicle, MSRP < $55K sedan / $80K SUV. Used EV credit up to $4,000.

chargingHome.notes: Level 2 (240V) home charger. Electricity ~$0.12-0.16/kWh. ~1,000 mi/month = ~300 kWh = $36-48/mo.

chargingPublic.notes: Tesla Supercharger ~$0.40-0.50/kWh. Electrify America ~$0.43/kWh. ChargePoint varies. Free L2 chargers at some retailers.

insurance.notes: EV insurance 15-25% higher than comparable ICE vehicle. Shop Liberty Mutual, Progressive, Tesla Insurance where available.

maintenance.notes: No oil changes, fewer brake jobs (regen braking). Tire rotation every 7,500 mi. Cabin air filter annually. Brake fluid every 2-3 yrs. 12V battery every 4-5 yrs (~$100). Tires wear faster on heavier EVs.

registration.notes: Many states add EV-specific fees ($100-275/yr) to replace lost gas tax revenue. Virginia: $64/yr EV fee.

electricVehicle.batteryWarranty: 8 years / 100,000 miles (federal minimum). Most manufacturers cover 70-80% capacity retention.
electricVehicle.depreciationNotes: EVs depreciate 40-50% in first 3 years, though Tesla holds value better. Used EV market improving. Budget ~$3,000-5,000/yr depreciation on $35K vehicle.
electricVehicle.totalMonthlyNotes: Includes: charging ($50), insurance ($180), maintenance ($50/mo avg), registration ($17/mo). Excludes: purchase/loan payment, depreciation.

discounts.name: Medicare
discounts.description: Federal health insurance at 65. Part A (hospital) premium-free for most. Part B (medical) ~$185/month. Part D (drugs) $10-50/month.

discounts.name: AARP Membership
discounts.description: Annual membership $16. Discounts on insurance, dining, travel, retail, and entertainment. Available at age 50+.

discounts.name: Virginia Social Security Tax Exemption
discounts.description: Virginia fully exempts Social Security benefits from state income tax. Additional $12,000 age deduction per person at 65+.

discounts.name: WMATA Senior Transit Fare
discounts.description: Half-price Metro fares during off-peak hours and all-day bus fares for riders 65+. Senior SmarTrip card required.

discounts.name: Fairfax County Senior Programs
discounts.description: Free or reduced-cost recreation programs, senior centers, health screenings, tax preparation assistance, and Meals on Wheels.

discounts.name: Virginia Personal Property Tax Relief
discounts.description: Some Virginia localities offer personal property tax relief for seniors meeting income requirements. Fairfax County offers tax deferral for 65+.

discounts.name: America the Beautiful Senior Pass
discounts.description: Lifetime pass to all national parks and federal recreation areas for $80 (or $20/year).

discounts.name: SilverSneakers Fitness
discounts.description: Free gym membership through participating Medicare Advantage and Medigap plans. Access to 17,000+ locations nationwide.

plans.name: T-Mobile 55+ Essentials
plans.notes: 2 lines unlimited talk/text/data. VA wireless tax ~9% + federal USF. 5G included.

breakdown.notes: Water, sewage, and trash are separate utility bills — see utilities breakdown.

housing.taxNotes: Virginia has no rental tax. Property tax (avg 1.15% Fairfax County) is landlord responsibility but built into rent. Renter's insurance recommended but not legally required.
housing.leaseTerms: 12-month standard lease. Security deposit max 2 months rent ($6,000). Pet deposit common ($300-500).
housing.marketNotes: Fairfax County/NoVA is one of the most expensive rental markets in the US outside major cities. 2BR houses are scarce; most rentals are apartments/townhomes. Expect competition.

stores.name: Wegmans
stores.notes: Popular in NoVA, great prepared foods

items.name: Chicken (whole/thighs)
items.notes: Primary protein for both adults & dog

items.name: Ground Turkey / Beef
items.notes: Lean ground for dog food base

items.name: Organ Meat (liver/heart)
items.notes: Essential for balanced home-cooked dog diet

healthcare.coverageSummary: Medicare Part A (free) + Part B ($174.70/mo pp) + Medigap Plan G (~$200/mo pp) + Part D (~$45/mo pp). IRA out-of-pocket cap $2,000/yr starting 2025.

costs.notes: Higher due to AMD + multiple conditions. Age-rated.
Medicare covers 80%, Medigap covers 20% coinsurance. ~6-8 injections/yr at $40-50 copay each.
OCT scans every 4-8 weeks. Medicare + Medigap covers most.
Quarterly bloodwork/visits. Low copay with Medigap.
Annual visit + as-needed. Mild asthma, infrequent.
Slightly lower — no AMD, but diabetes management
All generics — lower tier plan sufficient
Quarterly A1C, bloodwork. Covered well by Medicare.
Medicare Part B covers glucose monitors. Some copays.

shared.notes: Separate dental plan ~$35/person. Medicare does not cover dental.
Basic vision plan. AMD specialist visits covered by Medicare Part B.
Covered 100% by Medicare — no copay for preventive.

blackHairCare.label: Black Hair Care
blackHairCare.notes: Northern Virginia has a strong network of Black-owned salons, especially along the Route 1 corridor and in neighborhoods like Alexandria and Woodbridge.

providers.name: Progressions Salon Spa
providers.notes: Award-winning salon specializing in natural hair, locs, color, silk press. Black-owned.

providers.name: Hair Cuttery / Dominican Blowout Studios
providers.notes: Multiple stylists experienced with textured hair, color services, protective styles.

whiteHairCare.label: White/Caucasian Hair Care
whiteHairCare.notes: Abundant options throughout Fairfax County and Arlington. Many high-end salons in Tysons, Mosaic District, and Old Town Alexandria.

providers.name: Salon Nordine
providers.notes: High-end salon with color specialists, balayage, highlights.

providers.name: Salon deZEN
providers.notes: Personalized service, ammonia-free color options, experienced colorists.

mensBigTall.label: Men's Big & Tall Clothing
mensBigTall.notes: DXL is the primary brick-and-mortar option. Nordstrom at Tysons carries extended sizes. Online options (Amazon, King Size) ship quickly to the area.

providers.name: DXL Big + Tall
providers.notes: Dedicated big & tall retailer. Sizes up to 8XL and 72 waist. Brands: Polo, Nautica, Harbor Bay.

providers.name: Nordstrom (Tysons Galleria)
providers.notes: Extended sizes in-store and online. Good selection of dress shirts, suits in larger sizes.

womensClothing.label: Women's Regular Clothing
womensClothing.notes: Excellent shopping at Tysons Corner Center, Fair Oaks Mall, and Mosaic District. Wide range from budget (Target, Old Navy) to mid-range (Loft, Banana Republic) to upscale (Nordstrom).

providers.name: Nordstrom (Tysons Corner)
providers.notes: Full range of women's sizes (0-24+). Strong regular-size selection, good alterations department.

providers.name: LOFT / Ann Taylor (Mosaic District)
providers.notes: Regular and plus sizes. Work-to-weekend styles. Frequent 40-50% off sales.

shoes.label: Shoes
shoes.notes: Good selection for wide widths at DSW, Nordstrom, and New Balance stores. Tysons area has the best variety.

providers.name: DSW (Tysons / Fairfax)
providers.notes: Wide width options. Men's up to 16. Women's wide and extra-wide. Good clearance section.

providers.name: Nordstrom Rack (Fair Lakes)
providers.notes: Discounted name-brand shoes in extended sizes. Good for dress shoes and boots.
